title: Senghor: writings on politics [Book Review]
author: Manga

opening_move.description: The review opens by presenting Ripert's edition as a scholarly introduction to Senghor's political vision, praising the editor's command of the corpus and the quality of the English translations.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: Senghor's political writings deserve wider Anglophone attention as a contribution to postcolonial African political thought

theoretical_positioning.description: The review is appreciative and expository, presenting Senghor's political vision through Ripert's edition without substantive independent critique.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: intellectual history; close reading of Senghor's political writings
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review genre; primarily expository. Classified as purely_interpretive_hermeneutic given focus on presenting and contextualising Senghor's thought without normative argument.

case_type.case_name: Senghor, Liberté volumes 1-4; Ripert, ed., Senghor: Writings on Politics (2025)

confidence_notes: Book review genre; primarily expository and appreciative. Very low independent normative contribution.
